_comment: Documented account-balance endpoints, keyed by models.dev provider id.
Data, not code: an operator can add a provider here without touching Python.
Consumed by `model_verify.py --credit-probe`, which writes the verdict into
the schema-versioned credit cache that providers_resolve.py reads when it
applies the operator's credit rule (strongest PAID model when the account is
funded, strongest FREE model when it is not).
Only providers with a DOCUMENTED, first-party balance endpoint belong here.
Most providers publish none; for those the probe falls back to interpreting a
request against a paid model (402 / 'insufficient balance' => no credit,
200 => credit) and, failing that, reports 'unknown' — which the resolver
conservatively treats as no credit rather than guessing.
Entry fields:
url      absolute https endpoint (GET)
auth     bearer | x-api-key | raw (header named by auth_header)
doc      citation for the response shape below
signals  ORDERED list, most precise first; the first one PRESENT in the
response decides. Each signal has:
path  list of dict keys / integer list indices
type  balance          number (or decimal string) > 0 => funded
boolean          true  => funded
boolean_negated  false => funded
minus optional path subtracted from a balance (spent-to-date)
desc  what the field actually means, for the audit trail
Ordering matters: a field that is exact but often null must come before a
coarser field that is always present, or the coarse one would mask it.

signals.desc: DeepSeek's own verdict on whether the balance suffices for API calls
total balance (decimal string) for the first listed currency
credits left under this key's spending cap; null when the key is uncapped
false once the account has purchased credits; weaker than a live balance, so it is only consulted when limit_remaining is null
